report:
## Summary
Edgar, your performance last week was outstanding, achieving 63.4 hours, which is 133.5% of your weekly goal. This marks a significant improvement from your previous weeks, where you struggled to meet the goal, particularly in the weeks of April 11-17 and April 18-24, where you only reached 32.4 and 41.5 hours, respectively. Overall, your trend shows a strong upward trajectory in productivity.

## What's Working
- You achieved an impressive 63.4 hours during the week of April 25-May 1, showcasing your ability to maximize productivity when focused.
- Your pacing has improved notably, with a strong performance on Thursday, April 27, where you billed 14.2 hours.
- The last six weeks show a consistent pattern of improvement, with four out of the last six weeks exceeding the weekly goal.
- Your ability to complete complex jobs is evident; for example, you handled a significant amount of work on Friday, April 28, billing 4.5 hours despite it being a typically slower day.
- You have effectively managed your workload, as seen in the timely updates on your WIP, ensuring that customers are informed about their vehicle status.

## Where to Focus
- You have a few open ROs that are aging, particularly RO 776846 (14 days old) for the engine work. It’s crucial to prioritize this to avoid further delays and customer dissatisfaction. Aim to complete this by mid-week.
- Your billing on Fridays has been lower, with only 4.5 hours last week. Consider planning your schedule to take on lighter jobs or easier tasks on Fridays to maximize your output.
- While you’re doing well with high-priority ROs, there are still two high-priority jobs (RO 777092 and RO 777158) in the queue that need your attention. Picking these up can help maintain your strong performance.
- The week of April 18-24 showed a drop to only 41.5 hours. Reflect on what factors contributed to this dip, and strategize on ways to maintain consistent productivity throughout the week.
- Ensure that your communication with parts suppliers is proactive, especially for RO 777171, which has parts arriving on 5/2. Timely updates can help you complete jobs faster.

## Action Items for This Week
1. Prioritize completing RO 776846 (14d old) early in the week to prevent further delays.
2. Aim to increase productivity on Fridays by scheduling shorter, more manageable tasks or picking up additional jobs from the queue.
3. Immediately address the two high-priority ROs (777092 and 777158) to maintain momentum and ensure customer satisfaction.

## WIP Watchlist
- Focus on RO 776846 (14d old) for engine work to avoid it aging further.
- Keep an eye on RO 777171 (3d old) and ensure parts are ready for when they arrive on 5/2. 
- Nothing critical — keep moving.
